title: Ending Chemical Abortions Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill would make it a serious federal crime to give out or sell drugs that are used for chemical abortions. People who do this could go to prison for up to 25 years. The bill does not apply to birth control pills used before getting pregnant or medicine used to treat miscarriages or save a woman's life. Women who get chemical abortions would not be punished under this law.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Ending Chemical Abortions Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress states the following facts. In two thousand, the Food and Drug Administration approved chemical abortion drugs for use in the United States. The agency wrongly treated pregnancy as an illness and claimed chemical abortion drugs provide meaningful medical benefits. In two thousand sixteen, the Food and Drug Administration reduced the number of doctor visits needed for chemical abortion drugs from three visits to one visit. The agency also removed the requirement for doctors to give misoprostol in person and for follow up appointments. At this time, the agency also expanded the time period for causing a chemical abortion from seven weeks to ten weeks. In two thousand twenty one, the Food and Drug Administration eliminated the requirement that chemical abortion drugs be given in person. This allowed these drugs to be sent by mail in violation of long standing federal law. When compared to surgical abortions, chemical abortions are more likely to cause complications that are wrongly recorded as a spontaneous abortion or miscarriage. According to the Guttmacher Institute, the abortion industry's research organization, since two thousand, the use of mifepristone and misoprostol has grown to make up over fifty percent of all induced abortions in the United States. There is a four times higher risk of experiencing complications from a chemical abortion than from a surgical abortion.

sections.title: Section 3 Renaming Chapter 74 of Title 18, United States Code
sections.text: The table of chapters for part one of title eighteen of the United States Code is changed by removing the item related to chapter seventy four and adding the following. Chapter seventy four is now called Abortion crimes.

sections.title: Section 4 Chemical Abortions Prohibited
sections.text: Chapter seventy four of title eighteen of the United States Code is changed by adding a new section after section one thousand five hundred thirty one. The new section one thousand five hundred thirty two is called Chemical abortions. This section says that despite any other law, anyone who prescribes, dispenses, distributes, or sells any drug, medication, or chemical for the purpose of causing or performing an abortion on any woman shall be imprisoned for not more than twenty five years, fined under this title, or both. This section does not apply to the following situations. First, the sale, use, prescription or giving of any birth control agent used before conception or before pregnancy can be confirmed through regular testing. Second, the treatment of a miscarriage according to medical guidelines as accepted as of the date of the miscarriage. Third, in cases where a woman suffers from a physical disorder, physical injury, or physical illness, including a life threatening physical condition caused by or arising from the pregnancy itself, that would, as certified by a physician, place the woman in danger of death. A woman who has a chemical abortion performed on her or attempted on her may not be criminally prosecuted under this section. In this section, abortion means intentionally ending the pregnancy of a woman known to be pregnant, with an intention other than to produce a live birth, to remove a dead unborn child caused by miscarriage, or to treat an ectopic or molar pregnancy. Pregnant or pregnancy refers to the human female reproductive condition of having a living unborn child within her body throughout the entire embryonic and fetal stages from fertilization to full gestation and childbirth. Unborn child means an individual organism of the species homo sapiens, beginning at fertilization, until the point of being born alive as defined in section eight b of title one of the United States Code. The table of sections for chapter seventy four of title eighteen of the United States Code is changed by adding after the item relating to section one thousand five hundred thirty one the following. Section one thousand five hundred thirty two is called Chemical abortions.
